title: Weekly Surge In Laborjobs Google Trends Searches For Gig Economy Signals Bullish Xly
thesis: A surge in interest for gig economy jobs indicates increased consumer spending flexibility and labor market fluidity, benefiting consumer discretionary sectors. Higher gig economy interest often precedes consumer discretionary spending increases.
data_sources: Google Trends weekly search interest for 'gig economy'
signal_logic: If weekly Google Trends search interest for 'gig economy' rises more than 15% week-over-week
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly Google Trends search interest for 'gig economy' rises more than 15% week-over-week Exit: After 3 weeks or if interest drops below 5% above baseline
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Google Trends weekly search interest for 'gig economy' via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Gig economy interest fluctuates frequently with labor market changes and news cycles.

title: Daily Spike In Earthquakeactivity Near Key Freight Hubs Signals Bearish Xli

position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Earthquake activity near hubs is variable and can spike multiple times yearly.
